violations.finding: New diff (lines 103-108) adds inline Python scripting logic described in prose: instructs the agent to construct and run a Python script on the fly using inline expressions (`sha256(realpath(GIT_ROOT))[:16]`, `branch.replace('/', '__')`, `/tmp/agentic-commit/push/<repo_hash>`, etc.). This is executable Python logic embedded directly in the agent file rather than extracted to a named script in scripts/.
violations.recommendation: Extract the push-gate token writing logic to a dedicated script, e.g. `scripts/write-push-gate-token.sh` or `~/.claude/scripts/write-push-gate-token.py`, accepting GIT_ROOT, BRANCH, and COMMIT_SHA as parameters. Replace the prose description with a single-line invocation: `python ~/.claude/scripts/write-push-gate-token.py "$GIT_ROOT" "$BRANCH" "$COMMIT_SHA" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID"`.

violations.finding: New diff (lines 103-108) introduces a hardcoded temporary directory path `/tmp/agentic-commit/push/<repo_hash>` embedded in the agent file prose. This path is not derived from a parameter or environment variable within the agent instruction.
violations.recommendation: The path `/tmp/agentic-commit/push/` should be defined as a constant or environment variable in the extracted script (see no-inline-code recommendation). The agent file should not embed filesystem paths; it should reference the script by name only.

english-only (all agents/*.md and .claude/commands/*.md — no Chinese characters found; full unconditional scan performed per Standard 6 exception)

concise-documentation (commands/clean.md diff is a single-line simplification; agents/cleaner.md diff is concise prose, no verbosity or marketing language)
parameterized-scripts (not applicable — no new scripts introduced by this diff)
dangling-references (commands/clean.md:589 removes reference to /commit and retains /push — /push exists as hooks/push.sh; no broken references introduced)
merge-conflicts (no conflict markers in either file)
